zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Winter於大約1372年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾波丁頓時，她的父親William DeWinter23歲，母親Elizabeth Appleby23歲。她與Sir Richard Reed結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1412年在英國英格蘭享年41歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Winter于大约1372年出生在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾波丁頓时，她的父亲William DeWinter23岁，母亲Elizabeth Appleby23岁。她与Sir Richard Reed结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1412年在英国英格兰享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: Winter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old English) personal name Winter (Old English Wintra), originally a nickname from the word for ‘winter’ and perhaps still a nickname (see 2 below) in the period of surname formation.
English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name from Middle English winter, a variant of Middle English, Anglo-Norman French viniter, vinter ‘wine merchant’.
Irish: Anglicized form (part translation) of Gaelic Mac Giolla-Gheimhridh ‘son of the lad of winter’, from geimhreadh ‘winter’. This name is also Anglicized McAlivery.

variants.name: Winters
meanings: Dutch and German: patronymic from   Winter  .
English: variant of Winter , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: adopted for Mac Giolla Gheimhridh ‘son of the lad of winter’, from geimhreadh ‘winter’. Compare Winter 4.

variants.name: Winther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Niels, Aksel, Anders, Bent, Jorgen, Lars, Nels, Sven.  German Gerhardt, Kurt, Otto.
German, Danish, and Swedish: variant of   Winter  .

variants.name: Winterberg
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called with Middle High German winter ‘winter’ + berg ‘mountain, hill’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name composed of German Winter + Berg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Winterman
meanings: English (Kent and Middlesex): occupational name or status name denoting a servant of a man named   Winter   1.
English (Kent and Middlesex): alternatively, perhaps a nickname denoting a person with a cold, wintry appearance or disposition.

English (Kent and Middlesex): occasionally perhaps an occupational name for a wine merchant or a wine merchant's servant, from a variant of Middle English and Anglo-Norman French viniter, vinter ‘wine merchant’ (modern English vintner) + man ‘man’.

variants.name: De Winter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Urbain, Marcel.
Dutch; Flemish (also Dewinter): variant of Winter + definite article de, particularly as a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or tavern named with the season.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Winter was born about 1372, in Boddington, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William DeWinter, was 23 and her mother, Elizabeth Appleby, was 23. She married Sir Richard Reed. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1412, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Winter est née environ 1372, à Boddington, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William DeWinter, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Appleby, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Reed. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1412, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Winter um 1372 in Boddington, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William DeWinter 23 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Appleby 23. Sie hat Sir Richard Reed geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1412 im Alter von 41 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Winter è nata il circa 1372 nel Boddington, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William DeWinter, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Appleby, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Sir Richard Reed. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1412, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Winterが約1372年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアボッドイントンで生まれた時，父William DeWinterは23歳，母Elizabeth Applebyは23歳であった。Sir Richard Reedと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1412年にイギリスイギリスで41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Winter(이)가 대략 1372년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 보딩턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William DeWinter(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Appleby(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 Sir Richard Reed(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1412년 영국 잉글랜드에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Winter nasceu aproximadamente 1372, em Boddington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William DeWinter, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Appleby, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard Reed. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1412, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Winter родилась около 1372 г. в местности боддингтон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William DeWinter было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Appleby, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Reed. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1412 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Winter nació aproximadamente 1372, en Boddington, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William DeWinter, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Appleby, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Sir Richard Reed. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1412, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 41 años.
